text: interacting block, often good idea test block constructor worked intended created correct class object. example, let’s create pretend “test_block” class: Next, may want test input widgets block work intended. , changes inputs reflected changes reactive values block. example, let’s imagine test block input widget allows user select color: confirming input widgets work expected, may want check “state” block returned correctly. Let’s expand previous example add second input widget, number, allows user select numeric value. access state returned block indexing session$returned object returned shiny::testServer(): addition checking “state” returned block, can also check “expr” correctly evaluated. evaluate expressions, can call base::eval() returned expression, check evaluated expressions matches expression. example, let’s assume input widgets combine expression build new “colored_number” class just prints colored number screen: may also want test errors correctly thrown incorrect expressions: likely blocks require data blocks passed arguments block. test suite, can pass objects list args object testServer(). instance, let’s assume test block requires data.frame passed argument “df”:
code: test_that("block constructor", {     expect_s3_class(new_test_block(), "test_block") }) test_that("test block server input widgets update reactive values", {     testServer(         app = new_test_block()$expr_server, # Capture the expression server         expr = {             session$setInputs(color = "green") # Set a color             expect_equal(color(), "green")              session$setInputs(color = "red") # Change color             expect_equal(color(), "red")         }     ) }) test_that("state is correctly returned", {     testServer(         app = new_test_block()$expr_server,         expr = {             # First check default values             expect_equal(session$returned$state$number(), numeric())             expect_equal(session$returned$state$color(), character())              # Then toggle the inputs and recheck state is updated correctly             session$setInputs(number = 1)             expect_equal(session$returned$state$number(), 1)             session$setInputs(color = "pink")             expect_equal(session$returned$state$color(), "pink")         }     ) }) test_that("expr evaluates correctly", {     testServer(         app = new_test_block()$expr_server,         expr = {             session$setInputs(number = 1)             session$setInputs(color = "cyan")              # Call `base::eval()` on our expression             evaluated_expr <- eval(session$returned$expr())             expect_s3_class(evaluated_expr, "colored_number")         }     ) }) test_that("incorrect colours throw an error", {     testServer(         app = new_test_block()$expr_server,         expr = {             session$setInputs(number = 2)              # Set invalid colour             session$setInputs(color = "Ooops")              # Check that an error is thrown             expect_error(eval(session$returned$expr()))         }     ) }) test_that("test block server input widgets update reactive values", {     testServer(         app = new_test_block()$expr_server,         # Set block arguments with the "args" argument         args = list(df = reactive(gt::gt(mtcars))),         expr = {             session$setInputs(color = "green")             expect_equal(color(), "green")         }     ) })

Cleanup removed block, stack view now uses shiny’s public session$destroy(id) (requires shiny >= 1.14.0) rather reaching undocumented shiny internals tear module’s inputs, outputs observers. exported destroy_module() observe() trace hook captured per-module observers removed; call session$destroy(id) directly instead. Breaking (#202). board callback now gates block construction, evaluation rendering per-block reactiveVal channels receives visibility – required (blocks needed) visible (screen) – place single visible write-channel. Breaking front-ends. visibility bundle carries third channel, frozen, letting front-end freeze block’s inputs server-side: setting visibility$frozen[[id]](TRUE) – example locked board shows outputs hides controls – holds block’s expression, state readiness serialized state last editable values drops input trigger, forged Shiny.setInputValue (still fires block’s observer) reaches neither expression, block’s status, re-evaluation, saved board. Externally controllable inputs held – high-priority observer reverts write frozen – frozen block fully read-. Upstream-data-driven re-evaluation still runs, unfreezing resumes normal input handling (#231). background_construction_delay now accepts Inf, skipping background construction pass block built becomes required. Code export (“Show code”) marks every block required, exported script covers whole board; -screen block fully configured holds export back instead emitting broken code (#269). blockr_ser() accepts partial block-state snapshot: board block omitted blocks (mapped NULL) serializes constructor scope instead aborting length(blocks) == length(x). Saving board deferred construction, -screen blocks never built carry live state, longer fails – unbuilt blocks round-trip constructors rather dropped (#279). finite background_construction_delay, staggered builder now prioritizes -screen view: tick builds next block needed visible set rest backlog, switching view re-prioritizes construction toward now screen newly-visible blocks come progressively instead one blocking build (#275). staggered builder longer monopolizes event loop runs. tick’s pacing delay now begins just-built block flushed rather reactive graph still flushing, pending user input serviced within one tick instead behind entire backlog (#276). Captured block conditions longer glue-interpolated logged, block whose warning error text contains braces – e.g. {summary_fun} / {data} placeholders tidyr::pivot_wider()’s duplicate-value warning – longer aborts reactive “Failed evaluate glue component”. extends notify() toast path’s use_glue = FALSE treatment capture_conditions() handlers replay() methods (#268). Switching active panel view longer re-evaluates blocks whose needed status unchanged. block gates data inputs eval status per-block needed slot rather whole needed set, skips re-evaluation interpolated expression input data unchanged, switching panel view re-evaluates newly-visible block, entire shared upstream pipeline (#271). Board deserialization can degrade gracefully instead aborting whole load block restored – constructor providing package unavailable, payload reconstructed, round-trip class check fails. blockr_deser() blocks gains on_error argument ("abort" "drop"), defaulting blockr_option("deser_on_error", "abort") deployment can opt dropping offending blocks (warning) via options(blockr.deser_on_error = "drop") BLOCKR_DESER_ON_ERROR environment variable. Links stacks referencing dropped block pruned surrounding board still loads (#264). links$mod board update changes link different value (example switching merge_block’s input x y) now applies instead silently discarded. Folding modified link add accumulator base c() dropped links class accumulator empty, downstream link setup ran without /input arguments aborted missing subscript, rolling back whole update; now concatenates vec_c() (#287). req() – silent flow-control throw empty message – evaluated block’s conditions captured longer recorded block error, block merely currently needed longer flashes text-less red error band (false error count) deployed apps. Empty-message conditions filtered emptiness rather class, validate(need(x, "msg")) message still surfaces (#289).

title: blockr.core 0.1.3
text: CRAN release: 2026-07-12 Block-server construction now ordered visibility – -screen blocks upstream closure build first, rest background (option background_construction_delay, default 50 ms; 0 opts ). Blocks now carry eval status (dormant, waiting, unset, failed, ready) gates evaluation, rendering downstream data, block never evaluates missing inputs shows stale result. Code export (generate_code()) now waits every block settled, showing “board ready” note instead partial script. Block conditions (errors, warnings, messages) exposed tidy data frames via server$conditions board$conditions(); block server longer returns raw cond object. notify() helper gains glue log arguments literal (brace-safe) text skipping redundant logging. allow_empty_state argument new_block() now accepts structured list(input = ..., data = ...) form relax required inputs variadic minimum per input kind. Boards incoming requests now resolved app-level loader argument serve() (default local_loader()); drops process-global staging slot, get_serve_obj() restore_board()’s meta argument gone. Block- registry-contributed board options (e.g. table preview page_size, n_rows, filter_rows) now saved restored, reset defaults reload. Boards can deployed read-via server-enforced blockr.locked option, refuses every mutation set. Block registration metadata can now declared roxygen2 tags (@block, @blockArg, …), collected new block_registration_roclet(); extension packages register via exported register_package_blocks(). Block registry entries gain structured argument spec (new_block_args(), new_block_arg()) JSON-Schema-subset type descriptors (arg_string(), arg_enum(), …), read via block_metadata(); registry_metadata() deprecated. manage-links manage-stacks plugins longer flicker cell inputs, clobber staged edits, needlessly re-render board re-emit. Board accessors (board_blocks(), board_links(), board_stacks()) now pure reads, removing quadratic re-validation dominated large-board startup. New exported generic external_ctrl_vars() predicate has_external_ctrl() expose component’s externally controllable variables. New exported trim_rv() fully removes entries reactiveValues object (assigning NULL leaves key behind); unlinking variadic argument now drops outright. str_value() compact printer, matching utils::str() methods, now covers remaining domain classes containers. blockr_deser.list() method now forwards ... per-class deserializers, letting callers thread context nested deserializers.

title: blockr.core 0.1.2
text: CRAN release: 2026-04-28 blocks, keys must block_external_ctrl_vars(blk) — non-ctrl-able changes go rm + add. Ctrl-arg writes hit corresponding reactiveVal place; block_name (always treated ctrl-able) updates block’s registry attribute. links stacks, deltas merged onto current entry via new update_link() / update_stack() S3 generics. default methods reconstruct entry stored constructor, preserving sub-class attributes. Sub-class owners (e.g. dock_stack adding color) need register method constructor deviates convention (#175). block_external_ctrl_vars() always includes "block_name" — every block can renamed update(...) regardless external_ctrl opt-. default ctrl plugin panel renders block_name field every block card alongside opted-ctrl vars. block_supports_external_ctrl() (now TRUE every block) removed; gates used call simplified accordingly. default block_server.block now constructs block_name reactiveVal per block appends vars list passed ctrl plugin. Two guarded observers keep reactiveVal sync block’s block_name attribute board (one emits update(mod = ...) ctrl plugin writes rv; pulls registry-attr changes back rv). ctrl_block_server plugin signature unchanged — block authors custom ctrl plugins see uniform vars reactiveVals. blockr option attach_default_packages can set opt evaluating block expressions objects default packages directly available. Add ctrl_block() plugin external block control, allowing blocks driven programmatically outside standard block UI. Add clear_board() removing blocks/stacks board. Add bbquote() helpers (., ..) cleaner code generation via bquote()-based quasiquotation. Export custom_plugins() custom_options() easier board customization. Add block_metadata() retrieving per-block metadata attach block metadata defaults block objects. Export test utilities (blockr_test_exports(), new_mock_session(), export_safely()) use downstream package tests.

title: blockr.core 0.1.1
text: CRAN release: 2025-12-06 Add Block-level notifications via (optional) expr server return value component cond. Export get_board_option_value() make available current option settings via session$UserData. Introduce (optional) dependency thematic auto-style plots. Export toolbar_ui() takes case “core” toolbar UI component. Utility functions chr_ply() related, well miscellaneous utilities set_names(), coal(), etc. now exported use dependent packages. Export export_code() make easier third-party generate_code() plugin implementations. Use evaluate::evaluate() capture plots. Add new_fixed_block() applying fixed (.e. non-paramtetrized) transformation data input. Board server callbacks invoked additional argument session. Export assertion utilities is_string(), is_count(), etc. Improved ser/des, now includes package/constructor information board, blocks, stacks options. corresponding infra Board options now contain UI/server components provide options customization. Also blocks can require certain options available. Introduces block_render_trigger() control per block class re-render block output. Rework block notifications provide reactiveValues() object containing notification types separate components. Auto-ID generation can now customized default provided ids package (available). Use glue package logging/block notifications; add glue-based text block. New board restore mechanism based session$reload(). Improvements registry: block icons fixed set categories.
